headline: Nike (NKE) Q3 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: Since then, we have continued to take meaningful actions to improve the health, quality, and foundation of our business.  Because of the scale and breadth of the NIKE portfolio, that progress will not happen all at once.  One of the most important actions we took this quarter was further removing unhealthy inventory of our classic footwear franchises from the marketplace.

raw.headline: Nike (NKE) Q3 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
raw.summary: Since then, we have continued to take meaningful actions to improve the health, quality, and foundation of our business.  Because of the scale and breadth of the NIKE portfolio, that progress will not happen all at once.  One of the most important actions we took this quarter was further removing unhealthy inventory of our classic footwear franchises from the marketplace.

headline: NKE Q1 Deep Dive: Flat Sales, Margin Pressure, and Strategy Shift Shape Outlook
summary: Athletic apparel brand Nike (NYSE:NKE) met Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, but sales were flat year on year at $11.28 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.35 per share was 24.3% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Nike shares tumble after surprise sales drop forecast
summary: STORY: Nike shares tumbled in extended trade Tuesday, after it forecast a surprise drop in fourth-quarter sales. The sportswear giant blamed weakness in China and slow progress in clearing older inventory.That outlook sent its stock down more than 9%. Nike's revenue was flat at just under $11.3 billion in the third quarter, but just above analyst estimates.However, CFO Matt Friend projected a drop of up to 4% in current-quarter sales.Sales in China fell 10% and Friend said they are expected to fall 20% in the fourth quarter.He cited volatility from rising oil prices and ongoing fighting in the Middle East as factors.The company has struggled in its second-largest market, which accounts for 15% of annual sales.It has faced market share losses versus local competitors Anta and Li Ning.Nike is also expecting to end the fourth quarter with excess inventory.Under CEO Elliott Hill, it has pulled back promotions, stepped up innovation and refocused on core franchises like running.The move comes as the company works to reset the business after years of excess inventory and uneven demand across North America and China.

raw.headline: Nike shares tumble after surprise sales drop forecast
raw.summary: STORY: Nike shares tumbled in extended trade Tuesday, after it forecast a surprise drop in fourth-quarter sales. The sportswear giant blamed weakness in China and slow progress in clearing older inventory.That outlook sent its stock down more than 9%. Nike's revenue was flat at just under $11.3 billion in the third quarter, but just above analyst estimates.However, CFO Matt Friend projected a drop of up to 4% in current-quarter sales.Sales in China fell 10% and Friend said they are expected to fall 20% in the fourth quarter.He cited volatility from rising oil prices and ongoing fighting in the Middle East as factors.The company has struggled in its second-largest market, which accounts for 15% of annual sales.It has faced market share losses versus local competitors Anta and Li Ning.Nike is also expecting to end the fourth quarter with excess inventory.Under CEO Elliott Hill, it has pulled back promotions, stepped up innovation and refocused on core franchises like running.The move comes as the company works to reset the business after years of excess inventory and uneven demand across North America and China.
